title: Sovereign Quantum Race Starts Before Quantum Advantage - EE Times
why:
- Sovereign quantum computing has become a strategic priority for several countries, with investments already being made in quantum ecosystems and hardware even though the technology is still immature.
- Denmark has established QuNorth to administer a sovereign quantum computer, with its first user meeting held before the computer, Magne, is operational (expected end of 2026).
- The Danish sovereign computer project is funded by public and private entities (Danish sovereign wealth fund EIFO and the Novo Nordisk Foundation).
- Atom Computing will supply the hardware based on neutral atom technology, which is considered the most advanced and scalable quantum modality at this point, while Microsoft will provide the software.
